name: Langfuse
pricing_note: Free Hobby tier (50k units/mo); Core $29/mo, Pro $199/mo, unlimited users on paid tiers; MIT core fully self-hostable
one_liner: Open-source (MIT) LLM engineering platform combining tracing, prompt management, evals and datasets, widely used as the default self-hosted observability stack.

name: LangSmith
pricing_note: Free developer seat; Plus ~$39/seat/mo plus usage-based trace billing; self-hosting is enterprise-only
funding_note: LangChain raised $125M Series B (2025) at ~$1.25B valuation
one_liner: Closed-source tracing, evals and monitoring platform from the LangChain team, deepest integration with LangChain/LangGraph but usable via OTel from any stack.

name: Braintrust
pricing_note: Free developer tier; usage-based paid plans; enterprise hybrid/self-hosted data plane
funding_note: $36M Series A (a16z, 2024) + $80M Series B (ICONIQ, Feb 2026, ~$800M valuation)
one_liner: Eval-first AI engineering platform with logging, datasets, an LLM proxy and a purpose-built trace database (Brainstore), aimed at production regression-catching.

name: Arize Phoenix
pricing_note: Fully free OSS (Elastic License v2); hosted Phoenix Cloud has a free tier
funding_note: Backed by Arize AI ($70M Series C, Feb 2025)
one_liner: Open-source, OpenTelemetry-based tracing and evaluation library that runs locally or self-hosted, serving as the OSS on-ramp to Arize's enterprise platform.

name: Arize AX
pricing_note: Free tier; Pro and enterprise tiers with custom pricing
funding_note: $70M Series C (Adams Street, M12, Datadog; Feb 2025), ~$131M total
one_liner: Enterprise AI observability and evaluation platform extending Arize's ML-monitoring heritage to LLM and agent workloads at scale.

name: Helicone
pricing_note: Free tier + per-seat paid plans; reported in maintenance mode after Mintlify acquisition (Mar 2026)
one_liner: Proxy/gateway-based LLM logging with one-line setup, unified cost and latency visibility across providers; now under Mintlify ownership.

name: W&B Weave
pricing_note: Free tier on W&B cloud; usage-based ingestion pricing; enterprise self-hosting via W&B Server
one_liner: LLM tracing and evaluation toolkit from Weights & Biases, integrated with the broader W&B experiment-tracking ecosystem.

name: Galileo
pricing_note: Free tier added in 2025; enterprise pricing custom
one_liner: Evaluation and guardrails platform whose in-house Luna-2 small judge models target low-cost, low-latency scoring of agentic workloads.

name: Confident AI (DeepEval)
pricing_note: DeepEval framework free OSS (Apache-2.0); Confident AI cloud has free + paid tiers
one_liner: Pytest-style open-source LLM evaluation framework (DeepEval) with a hosted platform for benchmarking, regression testing and red-teaming (DeepTeam).

name: Maxim AI
pricing_note: Free tier; paid plans + enterprise in-VPC deployment
one_liner: End-to-end agent simulation, evaluation and observability platform pitched at cross-functional product and engineering teams.

name: TruLens
pricing_note: Free OSS (MIT); maintained under Snowflake after TruEra acquisition
one_liner: Open-source library for feedback-function-based evaluation and tracing of RAG and agent apps, now stewarded by Snowflake.

name: Traceloop (OpenLLMetry)
pricing_note: OpenLLMetry SDK free (Apache-2.0); Traceloop cloud has free + paid tiers
one_liner: Vendor-neutral OpenTelemetry instrumentation for LLM apps (OpenLLMetry) that ships traces to any OTel backend, plus a hosted monitoring platform.

name: Lunary
pricing_note: Free tier; per-seat paid plans; Apache-2.0 self-hosting
one_liner: Lightweight open-source LLM observability with tracing, analytics, prompt templates and PII masking, formerly known as LLMonitor.

name: Portkey
pricing_note: OSS gateway free (MIT); cloud free tier + paid plans; enterprise via Palo Alto Networks
funding_note: $15M Series A (Elevation, Feb 2026); acquired by Palo Alto Networks (May 2026)
one_liner: AI gateway routing 1,600+ models with built-in logging, cost tracking, caching and guardrails; observability comes as a side effect of the proxy layer.

name: PromptLayer
pricing_note: Free tier + per-seat paid plans
one_liner: Prompt CMS with visual versioning, release labels and A/B testing, positioned so non-engineers can edit and deploy prompts independently.

name: Humanloop
pricing_note: Service shut down Sept 2025 after Anthropic acqui-hire; listed for migration reference only
funding_note: Team acquired by Anthropic (2025); previously YC S20
one_liner: Former prompt management and evaluation platform; shut down September 2025 with official migration paths to W&B, PromptLayer and Agenta.

name: Langtrace
pricing_note: OSS self-host free; managed cloud with free + paid tiers
one_liner: OpenTelemetry-native open-source tracing and metrics for LLM apps and agent frameworks, with a managed cloud option.

name: Opik (Comet)
pricing_note: Apache-2.0 self-host free; Comet cloud free tier + paid plans
funding_note: Backed by Comet ML ($50M Series B, 2021)
one_liner: Open-source LLM evaluation and tracing platform from Comet, combining trace logging, eval metrics and CI-friendly test suites.

name: AgentOps
pricing_note: Free tier + paid plans; SDK is MIT OSS
one_liner: Session-replay style observability for AI agents with time-travel debugging, cost tracking and first-class integrations into agent frameworks like CrewAI and AutoGen.

name: LiteLLM
pricing_note: MIT OSS proxy free; enterprise tier adds SSO/audit features
one_liner: Open-source LLM proxy/SDK that normalizes 100+ providers behind the OpenAI format with per-key budgets, spend tracking and rate limits.

name: Datadog LLM Observability
pricing_note: Free up to 40k LLM spans/mo; Pro from ~$160/mo incl. 100k LLM spans, billed per span thereafter
one_liner: LLM and agent tracing inside the Datadog APM suite, attractive to teams already standardized on Datadog for infrastructure monitoring.

name: New Relic AI Monitoring
pricing_note: Included in New Relic usage-based pricing: 100GB free/mo, then ~$0.40-0.60/GB ingest plus per-seat fees
one_liner: AI/LLM monitoring layer in the New Relic APM platform tracking model latency, token cost and errors alongside conventional app telemetry.

name: OpenLIT
pricing_note: Apache-2.0, fully free self-hosted; no managed cloud tier
one_liner: OpenTelemetry-native open-source platform covering LLM tracing, GPU monitoring, guardrails and a prompt vault with one-line auto-instrumentation of 50+ providers.

name: SigNoz
pricing_note: OSS self-host free; cloud from $49/mo usage-based
one_liner: Open-source OpenTelemetry APM that handles LLM observability via standard OTel instrumentation rather than an LLM-specific SDK.

name: Pydantic Logfire
pricing_note: Free monthly span allowance, then usage-based per-span pricing; SDK is OSS
one_liner: OpenTelemetry-based observability service from the Pydantic team with first-class PydanticAI and Python ecosystem integration.

name: PostHog LLM Analytics
pricing_note: Generous free tier; usage-based pricing per LLM event
funding_note: YC W20; multiple rounds incl. Series D (2025)
one_liner: LLM cost, latency and trace analytics bolted onto PostHog's product-analytics platform, letting teams join AI telemetry with user behavior data.

name: HoneyHive
pricing_note: Free developer tier; enterprise plans with dedicated/in-VPC deployment
one_liner: Evaluation and observability platform for production agents with prompt versioning, A/B tests and OTel-based tracing.

name: Respan (formerly Keywords AI)
pricing_note: Free tier + paid plans
one_liner: Unified gateway-plus-observability control plane for tracing and evaluating agent behavior, rebranded from Keywords AI.

name: Laminar
pricing_note: OSS self-host (Docker) free; cloud free tier + paid plans
one_liner: Open-source observability for long-running AI agents that captures LLM calls, tool use and browser actions for step-level debugging and replay.

name: Phospho
pricing_note: OSS core; hosted tier with free plan
one_liner: Open-source text analytics on LLM app messages, clustering and scoring conversations to surface what users actually do.

name: Fiddler AI
pricing_note: Enterprise custom pricing; Trust Service / Guardrails sold as platform modules
one_liner: Enterprise AI observability vendor from the ML-monitoring era, now offering LLM scoring, guardrails and bias/fairness auditing.

name: WhyLabs (whylogs/LangKit)
pricing_note: Platform open-sourced under Apache-2.0 in Jan 2025; run on own infra
funding_note: AI2 spin-out; pivoted to open source in 2025
one_liner: Profile-based monitoring (whylogs) plus LangKit LLM metrics that summarize data locally so raw prompts never leave your infrastructure.

name: Dynatrace AI Observability
pricing_note: Usage-based within Dynatrace platform pricing
one_liner: GenAI and LLM monitoring within the Dynatrace APM platform, covering tokens, cost and service health for enterprises already on Dynatrace.

name: Grafana Cloud AI Observability
pricing_note: Free Grafana Cloud tier; usage-based paid; integrates OpenLIT instrumentation
one_liner: OTel-based GenAI observability solution on Grafana Cloud, built on open-source instrumentation rather than a proprietary SDK.

name: Sentry AI Agent Monitoring
pricing_note: Included in Sentry's free/team plans; usage-based span billing
one_liner: Agent-call tracing and error monitoring inside Sentry, giving app developers LLM visibility in the tool they already use for crash reporting.

name: MLflow (Tracing & GenAI)
pricing_note: Apache-2.0, free; managed versions via Databricks
funding_note: Linux Foundation project, primarily backed by Databricks
one_liner: The MLOps standard's GenAI extension: trace logging, LLM evaluation and prompt registry inside open-source MLflow 3.

name: TrueFoundry
pricing_note: Free tier; enterprise in-VPC deployment
one_liner: AI gateway and deployment platform with built-in request logging, cost attribution and rate limiting for enterprise Kubernetes environments.

name: Patronus AI
pricing_note: API free tier; usage-based eval pricing; enterprise custom
one_liner: Evaluation API and research-driven judge models (e.g. Lynx, Glider) for hallucination detection plus domain benchmarks like FinanceBench.

name: Gentrace
pricing_note: Free tier; per-seat paid plans; self-hosted enterprise option
one_liner: Collaborative LLM testing and eval platform emphasizing UI-driven experiments shared between engineers and subject-matter experts.

name: Atla
pricing_note: API free tier; usage-based pricing
one_liner: Builds dedicated evaluator/judge models (Selene) and agent error-analysis tooling rather than a full observability suite.

name: Promptfoo
pricing_note: CLI/library free (MIT); paid enterprise for red-teaming at scale
one_liner: Config-file-driven open-source CLI for prompt evals, regression testing and LLM red-teaming that runs in CI.

name: Ragas
pricing_note: Apache-2.0 OSS; hosted app in development by Exploding Gradients
one_liner: The de-facto open-source metric library for RAG evaluation (faithfulness, context precision/recall), used standalone or inside other platforms.

name: Giskard
pricing_note: OSS library free; LLM Evaluation Hub commercial
one_liner: Open-source testing framework that scans LLM apps for hallucination, injection and bias vulnerabilities, with a commercial evaluation hub.

name: Deepchecks
pricing_note: OSS package free; LLM evaluation SaaS with free + paid tiers
one_liner: Continuous validation suite from the ML-testing world extended to LLM apps, scoring outputs across versions from dev to production.

name: Evidently AI
pricing_note: Apache-2.0 library free; Evidently Cloud free tier + paid plans
one_liner: Open-source evaluation and monitoring library (100+ metrics) spanning tabular ML drift and LLM judge-based checks, with a managed cloud.

name: Athina AI
pricing_note: Free tier; per-seat paid plans; eval SDK is OSS
one_liner: IDE-style platform for prompt experimentation, evals and monitoring aimed at mixed technical/non-technical AI teams.

name: Future AGI
pricing_note: Free tier + paid plans
one_liner: Evaluation and observability platform with a focus on voice-agent simulation and programmatic re-scoring of historical scenarios.

name: Inspect AI
pricing_note: MIT OSS, free; no commercial tier
funding_note: Built by the UK AI Security Institute (government)
one_liner: Government-built open-source framework for rigorous LLM and agent evaluations, popular for safety benchmarks and sandboxed agentic tasks.

name: OpenAI Evals
pricing_note: MIT OSS framework; OpenAI platform also has a hosted Evals product billed via API usage
one_liner: OpenAI's original open-source eval framework and registry; largely superseded by the hosted Evals API but still a reference implementation.

name: Okareo
pricing_note: Free tier + paid plans
one_liner: Synthetic-user simulation that runs on every commit to catch regressions in agent tone, policy compliance, tool use and routing.

name: LangWatch
pricing_note: OSS self-host; cloud free tier + paid plans
one_liner: Open-source agent testing and observability built around the Scenario simulation framework, covering text, voice and adversarial tests.

name: Hamming AI
pricing_note: Custom/contact pricing
one_liner: Automated testing for voice agents that places thousands of simulated phone calls and scores transcripts against rubrics.

name: Vantage
pricing_note: Free tier; paid plans scale with tracked spend
one_liner: Cloud cost platform with native token-level ingest for OpenAI/Anthropic and an MCP server for querying AI spend from coding assistants.

name: CloudZero
pricing_note: Custom pricing tied to tracked spend
one_liner: AI unit-economics platform mapping LLM and GPU spend to cost per feature, per customer and per deployment.

name: Finout
pricing_note: Custom pricing
one_liner: FinOps platform whose MegaBill model folds LLM/API spend into the same cost-allocation views as cloud infrastructure.

name: Pay-i
pricing_note: Contact/usage pricing
one_liner: GenAI-specific FinOps tracking cost per request, per feature and per customer to give product teams real unit economics.

name: OpenMeter
pricing_note: Apache-2.0 OSS; cloud with free tier + usage pricing
one_liner: Open-source usage metering for AI/API products, commonly used to meter tokens for billing and internal chargeback.

name: Tokencost
pricing_note: MIT Python library; no commercial tier
one_liner: Lightweight open-source library maintaining an up-to-date price table for estimating prompt/completion costs across 400+ models.

name: Vellum
pricing_note: Free tier; reported paid plans from ~$25/mo, enterprise custom
funding_note: YC W23; $5M seed, later Series A
one_liner: Low-code platform for prompts, workflows, evaluations and deployments with environment management for product teams.

name: Agenta
pricing_note: MIT OSS self-host free; cloud free + paid tiers
funding_note: Named as an official Humanloop migration path by Anthropic
one_liner: Open-source prompt playground, registry and evaluation platform covering the prompt lifecycle from experimentation to deployment.

name: PromptHub
pricing_note: Free tier + per-seat paid plans
one_liner: Git-style prompt version control with branch/commit/merge semantics, runtime REST retrieval and CI/CD quality gates.

name: Latitude
pricing_note: OSS self-host; cloud free tier + paid plans
one_liner: Open-source prompt engineering platform with versioning, evals and agent-running infrastructure (PromptL).

name: Freeplay
pricing_note: Contact pricing; team-oriented plans
one_liner: Prompt management, testing and human-review workflows aimed at cross-functional product teams shipping LLM features.

name: Orq.ai
pricing_note: Free tier; paid plans + enterprise self-hosting
one_liner: Generative AI collaboration platform bundling prompt management, deployments, evals and observability for SaaS teams.

name: Langtail
pricing_note: Free tier + paid plans
one_liner: Spreadsheet-like prompt testing and deployment studio with assertions and security guardrails for smaller teams.

name: Coval
pricing_note: Contact pricing
one_liner: Simulation-first testing and replay for voice/chat agents, borrowing evaluation methodology from autonomous-vehicle testing.

name: Invariant Labs
pricing_note: OSS analyzer/Explorer; commercial via Snyk after acquisition
funding_note: Acquired by Snyk (2025); ETH Zurich spin-off
one_liner: Agent trace analysis and security scanning (incl. MCP tool-poisoning research), with an Explorer UI for debugging agent runs.

name: Guardrails AI
pricing_note: Apache-2.0 framework free; Guardrails Hub community validators; managed offering for enterprises
one_liner: Open-source output-validation framework where composable validators enforce schemas, policies and safety constraints on LLM I/O.

name: NVIDIA NeMo Guardrails
pricing_note: Apache-2.0, free; enterprise support via NVIDIA AI Enterprise
one_liner: Programmable conversational guardrails toolkit using the Colang DSL, covering input, dialog, retrieval, execution and output rails.

name: Lakera Guard
pricing_note: Free community API tier; enterprise SLA-backed plans
funding_note: Acquired by Check Point (2025); previously $30M raised
one_liner: Low-latency API guarding against prompt injection, data leakage and toxic content, backed by the Gandalf attack dataset.

name: LLM Guard (Protect AI)
pricing_note: MIT OSS scanners free; enterprise via Palo Alto Networks (Protect AI acquisition)
funding_note: Protect AI acquired by Palo Alto Networks (2025)
one_liner: Open-source input/output scanner toolkit (35+ scanners) for PII, injection and toxicity checks on LLM traffic.

name: Prompt Security
pricing_note: Custom enterprise pricing
one_liner: Enterprise GenAI security platform monitoring employee and application LLM usage for injection, leakage and shadow AI.

name: Cisco AI Defense (Robust Intelligence)
pricing_note: Enterprise pricing within Cisco security portfolio
one_liner: AI model validation and runtime guardrails productized from Robust Intelligence inside Cisco's security stack.

name: Arthur
pricing_note: Open-sourced engine components; enterprise platform custom-priced
one_liner: Model monitoring and firewall (Arthur Shield) for enterprise AI, focused on risk, bias and policy enforcement.

name: Coralogix AI (Aporia)
pricing_note: Usage-based within Coralogix platform pricing
one_liner: Aporia's drift detection and AI guardrails folded into the Coralogix observability platform as its AI research arm.

name: Haize Labs
pricing_note: Contact pricing
one_liner: Automated red-teaming ('haizing') that stress-tests LLM systems to find jailbreaks and failure modes before deployment.

name: CalypsoAI
pricing_note: Custom enterprise pricing
one_liner: Inference-perimeter security platform with scanners and red-team agents guarding enterprise model traffic.
